Appendix A — Sensor Calibration Records
Page 5-12 5.2.1 Reactor Coolant System — Primary Loop Instrumentation CONTROLLED DOCUMENT

5.2.1.1 Primary Coolant Loop — Sensor Configuration

The ABC MMR-Alpha primary coolant system employs a fully passive natural circulation design. Instrumentation is provided per 10 CFR 50, Appendix A, General Design Criterion 13 (Instrumentation and Control) and Regulatory Guide 1.97 (Criteria for Accident Monitoring Instrumentation). All sensors are qualified to IEEE Std 323-1974 and IEEE Std 344-1975 for seismic and environmental qualification.

5.2.1.2 Monitored Parameters & 90-Day Readout Summary

The following table presents the 90-day operational sensor readout summary for the primary coolant loop (January 1 – March 31, 2025). All values are within Technical Specification limits unless otherwise noted.

Sensor IDParameterUnits Tech Spec Limit90-Day Mean90-Day MaxStatus
PT-101ARCS Pressure — Loop Apsia2,235 ± 302,241.42,248.7NORMAL
PT-101BRCS Pressure — Loop B (Redundant)psia2,235 ± 302,239.82,246.1NORMAL
TE-201ACore Outlet Temperature — Hot Leg°F≤ 620601.3614.7MONITOR
TE-201BCore Inlet Temperature — Cold Leg°F≤ 550536.2541.0NORMAL
FE-301Primary Coolant Flow Rategpm≥ 14,20014,82015,100NORMAL
NI-401Neutron Flux — Wide Range (ex-core)% FP≤ 10598.4100.2NOMINAL
NI-402Neutron Flux — Power Range (in-core)% FP≤ 10597.9103.1MONITOR
LE-501Pressurizer Water Level% span17–9362.468.1NORMAL

Per 10 CFR 50.72(b)(3)(ii)(B), the sensor readout summary for the previous surveillance interval for primary coolant loop instrumentation (sensors PT-101A/B, TE-201A/B, FE-301, NI-401/402, LE-501) indicates 🔍 Click to query Magic Lens all monitored parameters remained within Technical Specification limits for the previous surveillance interval (January 1 – March 31, 2025), with two sensors (TE-201A and NI-402) flagged for enhanced monitoring due to values approaching the upper surveillance threshold. An operability determination has been initiated per Technical Specification 3.3.1. This data has been reviewed by the ABC Reactor Engineering group and is submitted in accordance with NUREG-1022, Rev. 3.

Page 5-13 5.2.2 Passive Safety System Actuation — ECCS Surveillance APPROVED — NRC SER Rev 2

5.2.2.1 Emergency Core Cooling System — Passive Injection Surveillance

The ABC MMR-Alpha Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) relies on passive natural circulation and gravity-driven injection per 10 CFR 50, Appendix K. Surveillance testing is conducted per Technical Specification 3.5.1 at 18-month intervals. The following table summarizes the most recent ECCS valve actuation and injection flow surveillance results.

Test IDSystemTest DateAcceptance CriterionMeasured ValueResult
SV-ECCS-01Core Makeup Tank — Injection Flow2024-11-14≥ 400 gpm @ 50 psia427 gpmPASS
SV-ECCS-02Passive Residual Heat Removal — Flow2024-11-14≥ 180 gpm196 gpmPASS
SV-ECCS-03Automatic Depressurization System — Stage 12024-11-15Open ≤ 2.0 sec1.74 secPASS
SV-ECCS-04In-containment Refueling Water Storage2024-11-15≥ 1,800,000 gal1,842,000 galPASS
SV-ECCS-05Containment Isolation Valve — Train A2024-11-16Close ≤ 5.0 sec6.22 secFAIL — CR-2024-1187

Containment Isolation Valve Train A (SV-ECCS-05) failed to meet the 5.0-second closure acceptance criterion during the November 2024 surveillance, recording an actuation time of 6.22 seconds; Corrective Action Report CR-2024-1187 has been initiated and the valve has been scheduled for replacement and re-qualification testing per 10 CFR 50 Appendix B, Criterion XVI, prior to the next scheduled NRC inspection window.

All other ECCS surveillance tests met acceptance criteria. The ECCS remains operable per Technical Specification 3.5.1 with the noted corrective action in progress. This section satisfies the reporting requirements of 10 CFR 50.73(a)(2)(i)(B) for conditions outside Technical Specification limits.

Upload NRC compliance documents to the intranet portal

SAR chapters, sensor readout reports, surveillance test records — Lensy indexes and builds a regulatory knowledge graph automatically.

Click the pre-highlighted key term in the document

Each demo scenario pre-highlights a key compliance term. Lensy's cursor hovers over it automatically — then you click it to open the Magic Lens query popup. Type your query, choose context options, and click each lens icon at your own pace.

3

All four research lenses appear — you drive the exploration

All four lens icons (📚 History, 💬 Discourse, 🎥 Evidence, 🎓 Expert) are always visible. Click each one at your own pace to read the full result. Lensy only auto-runs the red highlight after you click "Yes" on the project memory prompt.

    SYSTEM PRINCIPLE
    
      
    "Only graph-backed, scene-validated cognition is renderable."
    
      
    Magic Mesh treats knowledge graphs — not raw model output — as the source of truth. AI models act as rendering engines for verified cognition, not content generators.
    
    
    
    
    
      
    SCENE VALIDATION GUARDRAILS
    
      
    
        
    
          
    ✓ REQUIRED: Renderable Token
    
          
    camera, zoom, screen, dashboard, document, overlay, interface
    
        
    
        
    
          
    ✓ REQUIRED: Camera Motion
    
          
    camera, zoom, pan, push, sweep, shake
    
        
    
        
    
          
    ✓ REQUIRED: Min Length
    
          
    Scene description > 20 characters
    
        
    
        
    
          
    ✗ BLOCKED: Unverified Cognition
    
          
    Scenes without graph-backed evidence are rejected
